input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome!"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

        <Label Text="This page follows the system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads `Light` or `Dark` based on the OS theme and **switches automatically** at runtime — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# equivalent — `SetAppTheme` extension method:**

```csharp
public class MainPage : ContentPage
{
    public MainPage()
    {
        BackgroundColor = Colors.White;
        this.SetAppTheme(
            BackgroundColorProperty,
            Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
            Color.FromArgb("#1C1C1E"));

        var title = new Label { Text = "Welcome!", FontSize = 28 };
        title.SetAppTheme(
            Label.TextColorProperty,
            Color.FromArgb("#000000"),
            Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"));

        var button = new Button { Text = "Click Me" };
        button.SetAppTheme(
            Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
            Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
            Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
        button.SetAppTheme(
            Button.TextColorProperty,
            Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
            Color.FromArgb("#000000"));

        Content = new VerticalStackLayout
        {
            Padding = 20, Spacing = 16,
            Children = { title, button }
        };
    }
}
```

`SetAppTheme(BindableProperty, lightValue, darkValue)` does the same thing as the XAML markup extension.

---

**Key points:**

| Topic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Reacts automatically** | Both approaches listen to OS theme changes in real time |
| **Detect current theme** | `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` returns `AppTheme.Light`, `Dark`, or `Unspecified` |
| **Manual override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme |
| **Scalable approach** | For larger apps, define colors in a `ResourceDictionary` with `AppThemeBinding` and reference them with `{StaticResource}` / `{DynamicResource}` |
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a tutorial/explanation on how to use `AppThemeBinding` in .NET MAUI to support light/dark mode theming. This is a knowledge question — they want to see XAML examples and understand the C# alternative. I don't need to search a codebase; I can answer directly.
